stem:
Let \( \mathbf{F}(x,y,z) = \left( [[a]]y^{2}z,\; [[b]]z^{2}x,\; [[c]]x^{2}y ight) \). For ANY vector field with continuous second partial derivatives, \( 
abla\cdot(
abla	imes\mathbf{F}) \) is identically \( 0 \) (divergence and curl are the well-typed compositions scalar-from-vector and vector-from-vector, and the composite identity holds everywhere). Evaluate \( 
abla\cdot(
abla	imes\mathbf{F}) \) at the point \( ([[x0]],[[y0]],[[z0]]) \).
solution:
Writing \( 
abla	imes\mathbf{F} = (\partial_yF_3-\partial_zF_2,\ \partial_zF_1-\partial_xF_3,\ \partial_xF_2-\partial_yF_1) \), the divergence of this is \( \partial_x\partial_yF_3-\partial_x\partial_zF_2 + \partial_y\partial_zF_1-\partial_y\partial_xF_3 + \partial_z\partial_xF_2-\partial_z\partial_yF_1 \). Since mixed partials commute (\( \partial_x\partial_yF_3=\partial_y\partial_xF_3 \), etc.), every pair of terms cancels, so \( 
abla\cdot(
abla	imes\mathbf{F}) = 0 \) at every point, including \( ([[x0]],[[y0]],[[z0]]) \): \( [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This computes only one component of the curl's contribution and stops, instead of recognizing the full sum of mixed-partial pairs cancels to \( 0 \).
This adds all three curl-derivative terms as if they reinforced each other, instead of recognizing they cancel in pairs by equality of mixed partials.
This keeps only two of the three cancelling pairs and drops a sign, instead of recognizing the total is \( 0 \).
This is a small nonzero value; the divergence of a curl is identically \( 0 \) at every point.
This is a small nonzero value; the divergence of a curl is identically \( 0 \) at every point.
This is a small nonzero value; the divergence of a curl is identically \( 0 \) at every point.
This is a small nonzero value; the divergence of a curl is identically \( 0 \) at every point.

stem:
Let \( f(x,y,z) = [[a]]xyz^{2} + [[b]]xy^{2}z \). For ANY scalar field with continuous second partial derivatives, \( 
abla	imes(
abla f) \) is identically the zero vector (gradient produces a vector from a scalar, and curl of that vector is again well-typed, with the composite always vanishing). Evaluate the \( x \)-component of \( 
abla	imes(
abla f) \), namely \( \partial_y\!\left(\dfrac{\partial f}{\partial z}ight) - \partial_z\!\left(\dfrac{\partial f}{\partial y}ight) \), at the point \( ([[x0]],[[y0]],[[z0]]) \).
solution: Here \( \partial f/\partial z = 2([[a]])xyz + ([[b]])xy^{2} \), so \( \partial_y(\partial f/\partial z) = 2([[a]])xz + 2([[b]])xy \). Also \( \partial f/\partial y = ([[a]])xz^{2} + 2([[b]])xyz \), so \( \partial_z(\partial f/\partial y) = 2([[a]])xz + 2([[b]])xy \). These are the SAME mixed partial \( \partial^{2}f/\partial y\,\partial z \) computed in the two orders, so their difference is \( 0 \) at every point, including \( ([[x0]],[[y0]],[[z0]]) \): \( [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This adds the mixed partial to itself instead of subtracting it from itself, doubling a value that should cancel to \( 0 \).
This computes each partial with a coefficient dropped, giving a spurious nonzero difference instead of recognizing they are identical.
This keeps only the first mixed-partial term and drops the second entirely, instead of recognizing they cancel.
This is a small nonzero value; the curl of a gradient is identically \( 0 \) at every point.
This is a small nonzero value; the curl of a gradient is identically \( 0 \) at every point.
This is a small nonzero value; the curl of a gradient is identically \( 0 \) at every point.
This is a small nonzero value; the curl of a gradient is identically \( 0 \) at every point.

stem:
Let \( \mathbf{F}(x,y,z) = \left( [[a]]x^{2}y,\; [[b]]y^{2}z,\; [[c]]z^{2}x ight) \). Since \( 
abla\cdot\mathbf{F} \) is a well-typed SCALAR field, it may itself be differentiated by \( 
abla \) (unlike, say, \( 
abla(
abla	imes\mathbf{F}) \), which is not well-typed since \( 
abla \) needs a scalar input). Compute the \( x \)-component of \( 
abla(
abla\cdot\mathbf{F}) \) at the point \( ([[x0]], [[y0]], [[z0]]) \).
solution: First \( \nabla\cdot\mathbf{F} = \partial_x([[a]]x^{2}y) + \partial_y([[b]]y^{2}z) + \partial_z([[c]]z^{2}x) = 2([[a]])xy + 2([[b]])yz + 2([[c]])zx \), a genuine scalar field. Its \( x \)-partial is \( \partial_x(\nabla\cdot\mathbf{F}) = 2([[a]])y + 2([[c]])z \); at \( (1,[[y0]],[[z0]]) \) this is \( [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This drops the factor of 2 from differentiating the quadratic terms of the divergence.
This differentiates the ORIGINAL field components directly (using their squared-variable exponents) instead of first forming \( \nabla\cdot\mathbf{F} \) and then differentiating that.
This has the wrong overall sign.
This is half the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.
